name: Harvard Theatre Collection Posters
description: This collection includes over 2,000 posters from the Harvard Theatre Collection, featuring a variety of theatrical performances and events. Formats include digital images of original posters.

name: Museum of Modern Art (MoMA) Posters
institution: The Museum of Modern Art, New York
description: A comprehensive collection of posters from the MoMA archives. The collection features thousands of items, including high-resolution images and detailed metadata.

name: WWI Propaganda Poster Collection
description: This digital collection features over 1,700 posters from World War I, covering a range of topics related to the war effort. Formats include high-resolution images and detailed descriptions.

name: New York Public Library Digital Collections: Posters
description: A diverse collection of posters from the New York Public Library's archives, featuring over 40,000 items related to various themes including social issues, travel, and art.

name: Victoria & Albert Museum Textile Collection
description: A comprehensive collection of textiles from around the world, including costumes, tapestries, and embroideries. Contains over 80,000 items dating from ancient times to the present day.

name: Textile Museum Digital Collections
description: Includes a variety of textile objects such as rugs, clothing, and fabric samples. Over 10,000 items from around the world dating back to ancient times.

name: The Miriam and Ira D. Wallach Division of Art, Prints and Photographs: Photography Collection
description: This collection includes over 800,000 photographs from the 19th to the 21st century, featuring works by renowned photographers and covering a wide range of subjects including landscape and urban photography.

name: American Landscape and Architectural Design, 1857-1942
description: A collection featuring over 300 photographs and prints that document the evolution of American landscapes, cityscapes, and architectural designs.

name: The History of Science Collections: Scientific Instruments
institution: Harvard University Library Open Collections Program
description: This collection features historical scientific instruments, including diagrams and descriptions of their use. It contains over 40 items from the late 19th to mid-20th century.

name: Biodiversity Heritage Library
institution: Global Consortium of Natural History and Botanical Libraries
description: Offers over 180,000 volumes of historical texts in biodiversity. Includes thousands of botanical illustrations and scientific drawings.

name: University of Michigan Libraries Digital Collections - Botany & Botanical Illustration
description: Features a variety of botanical illustrations and scientific drawings from the 18th to 20th centuries. Includes over 10,000 items in various formats.

name: The Internet Archive Audio Collection
description: A vast collection of free audio books, poetry, music, and spoken word recordings from around the world. Contains over 200,000 items in various formats including MP3, WAV, and FLAC.

name: University of California, Santa Barbara Cylinder Audio Archive
description: A collection of historical recorded sound from the late 19th to the early 20th century, featuring over 8,400 cylinders and 600 recordings.

name: National Jukebox (Library of Congress)
description: Historical recordings from the Victor Talking Machine Company, featuring over 10,000 recordings of 78-rpm discs. Includes classical music, jazz, popular songs, and spoken word.

name: Science & Technology Collections
description: The Cambridge University Digital Library includes scientific instruments and diagrams, with a focus on historical and modern science. The collection features over 20,000 images in various formats.

name: The Science Museum Group Collection Online
description: This archive includes over 325,000 items related to science and technology, including scientific instruments, diagrams, and photographs. The collection spans multiple eras.

name: Costume Institute Fashion Plate Collection
description: This collection contains over 16,000 fashion plates from the late 18th to early 20th century. The digital archive includes high-quality images and comprehensive descriptions of each plate.

name: Fashion Plates from the New York Public Library
institution: New York Public Library Digital Collections
description: The collection includes over 1,500 fashion plates from the late 19th to early 20th century, showcasing various designs and styles of clothing.

name: The Image of the City: Photographs by Berenice Abbott
description: This collection contains over 700 photographs taken by Berenice Abbott of New York City, documenting the city's architecture and urban life from the 1930s.

name: Digital Public Library of America
description: The DPLA provides access to over 30 million digital objects from libraries, archives, and museums across the United States. The collection includes a wide range of formats such as photographs, books, maps, news footage, oral histories, personal letters, museum artifacts, artwork, government documents, and more.

name: MIT Libraries Visual Collections
description: The MIT Libraries Visual Collections include over 50,000 images from the university's archives and special collections. The collection features photographs, illustrations, architectural drawings, and more.

name: National Gallery of Art - NGA Images
description: Over 50,000 digital images of public domain artworks from the National Gallery of Art collection.

name: Astronomical Plate Collection
description: This collection includes over 30,000 photographic plates from the University of Michigan's Department of Astronomy, dating from the late 19th to the mid-20th century.

name: Folk Art Digital Collection
description: A collection of over 10,000 images from the University of Michigan’s Folk Art collection, including photographs and drawings from various folk art traditions around the world, spanning a wide range of eras.

name: Folk Art Collection
description: The National Museum of American History's Folk Art collection includes over 10,000 objects, ranging from quilts and textiles to wooden carvings and paintings. The digital archive provides access to images and descriptions.

name: Folk Art of Latin America and the Caribbean
description: This collection includes thousands of images, manuscripts, and audio recordings documenting folk art traditions in Latin America and the Caribbean. The materials span various formats and eras.

name: Digital Folk Art Collection
institution: Smithsonian Institution Archives of American Art
description: The Archives of American Art at the Smithsonian Institution contains a vast collection of folk art, including photographs, documents, and artwork. It includes over 80,000 items from the mid-19th century to the present.

name: National Library of Medicine Digital Collections
description: Offers a vast array of digitized medical illustrations, manuscripts, and historic images. Includes over 800,000 items in various formats from the Middle Ages to the present.

name: Wellcome Collection Digital Archive
description: A diverse collection of over 30,000 items including medical illustrations, manuscripts, and photographs. It spans various eras from the Middle Ages to modern times.

name: Norman B. Leventhal Map Center Collection
description: One of the largest collections of maps in a public library, featuring over 200,000 items including atlases, globes, and maritime charts.

name: Digital Sanborn Maps Collection
description: Over 25,000 large-scale maps of cities and towns in the United States from 1867 to 1970.

name: Historic Map Works Digital Collection
description: Thousands of historical maps from around the world, including antique atlases and rare maps.

name: USGS National Geologic Map Database
description: A comprehensive collection of geologic maps, reports, and related data. Includes over 90,000 items in various formats such as PDFs, images, and GIS files.

name: British Geological Survey (BGS) OpenGeoscience
description: An extensive collection of geological maps, data, and publications from the UK. Includes over 250,000 items in various formats such as PDFs, images, and GIS files.

name: Geological Society of London's Lyell Collection
description: A digital archive of the Geological Society of London's journals and special publications, including over 30,000 articles and maps.

name: Library of Congress: Broadside Collections
description: A vast collection of broadsides, including political cartoons, advertisements and more. Includes over 15,000 items, dating from the 17th century to modern times.

name: University of Michigan Poster Collection
description: More than 40,000 posters from various eras and regions, including political, social, and cultural themes.

name: University of California San Diego Digital Collections
description: Collection includes over 10,000 posters covering a wide range of topics from political activism to pop culture.

name: The Poster Collection at the National Gallery of Art
description: A collection of nearly 2,000 posters spanning various eras and themes, with a focus on art and culture.

name: Digital Public Library of America (DPLA) Design Collection
description: A vast collection of digital materials from libraries, archives, and museums across the United States, including thousands of design-related items.

name: The Wellcome Collection Online Gallery
description: Features a vast array of medical and scientific images, including drawings, illustrations, photographs, and artworks from the 16th century to the present.

name: The British Museum Image Collection
description: Offers access to over two million records, including drawings, illustrations, and maps from ancient civilizations to the 20th century.

name: The Library of Congress Digital Collections
description: Holds a vast array of digital collections, including illustrations and drawings from the 15th century to the present.

name: Solar System Exploration Multimedia Gallery
description: Features a wide range of images, videos, and audio files from various NASA missions exploring the solar system. Thousands of assets including Mars rovers, moon landings, and more.

name: The Apollo Image Gallery
institution: University of Texas at Austin - Briscoe Center for American History
description: A comprehensive collection of images from the Apollo missions, including photos taken on the moon and in space. Over 10,000 high-resolution images.

name: Zine Collection
description: The Internet Archive's zine collection contains a vast array of digital and scanned zines, including over 10,000 items in various formats from the 1960s to the present day.

name: Costume Institute Fashion Plate Collection
description: A collection of fashion plates from the 18th to early 20th century, featuring hand-colored prints and engravings that showcase various styles. Includes around 60 items.

name: Bibliothèque nationale de France - Costume Plates
description: The Bibliothèque nationale de France offers a collection of fashion plates from the 19th century. Includes around 10,000 items.

name: Fashion Plates at the New York Public Library
description: A digital archive of fashion plates from the 18th and 19th centuries, featuring a variety of clothing styles and accessories. Includes around 200 items.

name: Fashion Plates at the Victoria and Albert Museum
description: The V&A Museum's digital archive of fashion plates from the 18th to early 20th century, featuring detailed illustrations of clothing and accessories. Includes around 50 items.

name: Visual Arts Data Service (VADS)
description: A national online resource for visual arts, providing access to over 140,000 images, covering a wide range of media including architecture. The collection includes high-quality images and documents.

name: The Royal Society Digital Collections
description: This collection includes scientific instruments, diagrams, and manuscripts dating back to the 17th century. It contains over 50,000 items including journals, letters, and drawings.

name: Folk Art and Material Culture Collection
institution: New York Public Library Digital Collections
description: Includes rare books, manuscripts, photographs, and artifacts documenting folk art traditions from around the world. Over 1000 items.

name: Folk Art and Material Culture of Latin America Collection
description: Features digitized images, texts, and audio recordings related to the folk art and material culture of Latin America. Over 500 items from various eras.

name: Map Collections at the Library of Congress
description: The Library of Congress holds over 5 million maps, with a wide range of topics and eras. The collection includes digital images, geographical information system (GIS) data, and high-resolution scans.

name: World Digital Library Maps Collection
description: The World Digital Library includes a variety of maps from around the world. The collection features over 10,000 items, including high-resolution images of historical maps in various formats.

name: Digital Collections at Harvard University - Botanical Illustations Collection
description: A collection of over 20,000 botanical illustrations from the 17th to 21st century. Includes plates and specimens.

name: Natural History Museum at the Smithsonian Institution's Ornithology Collection
description: A vast collection of ornithological specimens and illustrations, including historic bird plates. Contains thousands of items in various formats.

name: European Folk Music Archive
description: A comprehensive collection of European folk music, featuring recordings from various regions and eras. The archive includes over 30,000 items, primarily in audio format.

name: The Library of Congress American Folklife Center Collection
description: This extensive digital archive features a vast array of folk art, vernacular design, and cultural heritage materials. It includes audio recordings, photographs, manuscripts, and field notes from various folklore projects across the United States.

name: The University of Wisconsin Digital Collections
description: This collection offers a diverse range of folk art and vernacular design items, including photographs, manuscripts, and artifacts. It features over 20,000 items in various formats, focusing on Wisconsin's cultural heritage.

name: The British Library Online Gallery
description: This collection includes a wide range of folk art and vernacular design items, including manuscripts, photographs, and prints. It features over 50,000 downloadable images in various formats, spanning multiple eras of British history.

name: Smithsonian Institution Libraries Digital Collections
description: Includes a variety of natural history specimens and plates, with over 1 million items ranging from illustrations to photographs.

name: University of Florida Digital Collections: Entomology and Nematology Department Collection
description: This collection includes historical insect illustrations, scientific drawings, and photographs from the Entomology and Nematology Department, with over 1000 digitized items.

name: Botanical Illustrations from the LuEsther T. Mertz Library
description: Featuring botanical illustrations, this collection includes over 40,000 images of plant species, botanical art, and historical documents in various formats. It covers the 18th to 20th centuries.

name: University of Florida Digital Collections - Florida Natural History Illustrations
institution: University of Florida George A. Smathers Libraries
description: This collection includes nearly 1,000 images of botanical and natural history illustrations from the 18th to early 20th centuries in various formats.

name: The National Film Board of Canada Online Collection
description: This extensive collection features over 3,000 films and animations available for streaming. It includes works from renowned animators like Norman McLaren and Co Hoedeman.

name: The Animation Archive at the Academy Film Archive
institution: Academy of Motion Picture Arts and Sciences
description: This archive contains a vast collection of animation films, scripts, storyboards, and other materials from the early days of animation to contemporary works.

name: The Digital Collections at the University of Southern California Libraries
description: This collection includes a variety of materials related to animation and motion graphics, such as scripts, production notes, and digital assets.

name: The Animation Resources Collection
description: This curated digital archive features a vast collection of animation materials, including rare books, drawings, and films from the early days of animation.

name: John James Audubon's Birds of America
description: This digital collection features the complete set of Audubon's Birds of America, including 435 plates. High-resolution images allow for detailed examination.

name: Birds of North America Collection
institution: National Museum of Natural History, Smithsonian Institution
description: A comprehensive collection featuring images and illustrations from the 19th century to present. Includes field guides, ornithological plates, and more.

name: Biodiversity Heritage Library Digital Art Collection
description: A vast collection of digitized ornithological art and scientific illustrations from books spanning the 18th to early 20th centuries, featuring over a million pages.

name: John James Audubon Collection
institution: The New York Public Library Digital Collections
description: This collection includes prints, drawings, and letters by John James Audubon, featuring bird illustrations as well as other natural history materials.

name: Cornell Lab of Ornithology’s Macro Gallery
description: The Cornell Lab of Ornithology's Macro Gallery features a vast collection of high-resolution images and videos of birds, including ornithological plates and bird art.

name: National Geological Repository
description: The National Geological Repository holds over 3 million rock, fossil and mineral samples from UK onshore wells, boreholes and field sites. It includes geological maps, reports, and other documentation.

name: Stanford University Geospatial Center Maps Collection
description: The Stanford Geospatial Center Maps Collection contains thousands of maps from various sources, including geological surveys and historical maps.

name: Harvard Art Museums Collections Online
description: Features over 250,000 artworks from the Harvard Art Museums' collections, including paintings, sculptures, and photographs.

name: Posters from the University of California, San Diego (UCSD)
institution: University of California, San Diego Libraries
description: The UCSD Library's digital archive features over 1000 posters spanning the mid-20th century to present day.

name: Rare and Historical Printed Materials at the University of Michigan Digital Library
description: The collection includes broadsides, posters, and other printed materials from the 16th to the 20th century.

name: American Antiquarian Society's Digital Collections
description: A comprehensive digital archive featuring a wide range of American ephemera and printed matter, including brochures, pamphlets, maps, and other historical documents from the 17th to the early 20th century.

name: University of Michigan's Digital Collections
description: The University of Michigan Library's digital collections include a variety of ephemeral materials, historical documents, and printed matter.

name: Medical Heritage Library
description: A digital collection of historic medical texts and illustrations. Includes over 65,000 items in various formats such as books, journals, prints, and photographs from the 15th to the early 20th century.

name: Digital Library of the Caribbean (dLOC)
description: Offers a rich collection of digitized materials related to folk art and vernacular design across the Caribbean. Includes books, photographs, maps, and more.

name: Folk Art Collection
description: Features a diverse array of folk art and vernacular design artifacts, including photographs, manuscripts, and audio recordings from the Southwest United States.

name: University of Washington Libraries Digital Collections
description: This collection features a variety of landscape and urban photography from the Pacific Northwest. It includes over 3,000 items in various formats, capturing scenes from the late 19th century to the present.

name: New York Public Library Digital Collections
description: The New York Public Library's digital collections include a vast array of urban and landscape photographs, with over 800,000 items spanning from the 19th century to the present. Formats include prints, negatives, and digital images.

name: New York Public Library Digital Collections: Fashion Plates
description: A collection of fashion plates from the 19th century, featuring over 2,000 hand-colored images showcasing various styles and trends. Items are in image format.

name: Fashion History Timeline: Digital Collection
institution: The Fashion Institute of Design & Merchandising
description: A comprehensive digital archive featuring over 700 years of fashion history, including detailed images and descriptions of garments from the Middle Ages to the present day. Items are in image format.

name: Bancroft Library Book Arts Collection
description: A diverse collection featuring over 10,000 items related to the art of bookmaking, including rare books, artists' books, and print ephemera from various eras.

name: Library of Congress Prints & Photographs Online Catalog
description: This extensive collection includes over 1 million images, featuring prints, photographs, and other visual materials from various historical periods, including book arts.

name: National Library of Scotland Digital Gallery
description: A comprehensive collection featuring over 10,000 items including rare books, manuscripts, maps and photographs. The book arts section includes printed materials showcasing the history of printing.

name: University of Michigan Digital Collections
description: The University of Michigan's digital collections include the Making of America (MOA) collection, which consists of over 8,500 digitized books and 100,000 journal articles from the 19th century. It includes ethnographic studies among other topics.

name: Cultural Heritage Online Digital Collections of the Americas
institution: The University of Florida Library and Latin Americanist Research Resources Project
description: A collection of digitized cultural heritage materials from around the world, including ethnographic collections. The database includes over 20,000 books, articles, images, and documents on a variety of topics.
